what_it_does: Backfills the agent-session registry from Claude Code catalog files under ~/.claude/projects/<project>/<uuid>.jsonl into the company store as closed registry records, using a read-only, streaming, event-free, idempotent process.
observations: Opens every main session jsonl in read-only text mode and streams it line-by-line, never loading the whole file.
Collects ai-title and last-prompt with overwrite-on-encounter to implement last-occurrence-wins semantics in a single forward pass.
Maintains an explicit title fallback chain: ai-title, last-prompt, first real user turn, then an untitled envelope-derived title.
Skips files whose name starts with agent- or that live in subagents/, treating them as sidechains rather than sessions.
Checks idempotency by comparing jsonl_bytes and jsonl_mtime against an existing record; --force bypasses this.
Never overwrites a record whose state is not closed, preserving live supervisor-owned records.
Detects CC-internal summarizer one-shots via a prefix match and marks the record with summarizer:true.
Tolerates malformed JSON lines, counting them in stats and continuing rather than failing the whole file.
Collects per-file errors and exits non-zero if any file failed or nothing was imported/was previously imported.
Imports fabric.config and FsStore inside main rather than at module top.
standouts: The strict read-only and no-event-emission laws are documented as hard constraints and reflected in the implementation.
The deterministic title fallback chain guarantees 100% titled records without inventing fields.
The summarizer flag is an additive, filterable marker for machine-generated summary sessions.
Duplicate session UUIDs across project directories are resolved by keeping the one with the later last_activity.
conventions: Heavy module docstring stating contracts, measurements, and audit criteria.
Module-level constants for noise prefixes, title max length, and title source taxonomy.
Type-hinted helper functions and a main() returning an integer exit code.
Use of a store abstraction (FsStore) for registry persistence.
UTC ISO-8601 timestamps and os.stat-derived size/mtime for idempotency.
Fail-loud error collection with non-zero exit codes.
concerns: The duplicate-resolution path checks sid in written_activity before the live/unchanged guard, so a second occurrence of a UUID could bypass the existing-record state check if a closed record was already written this run.
A project subdirectory entry named like a .jsonl file but actually a directory would be treated as a main file and produce a file error.
turns counts every user and assistant line as a turn, which may not match a conversational turn definition.
The broad except Exception in run_import swallows all per-file failures; while intended, it also hides the specific line or context where an error originated.
notes: A careful, boundary-respecting backfill tool that treats the Claude Code catalog as read-only history and the registry as a separate, closed-record-only target.
questions: What are the exact semantics of FsStore.save_agent_session and load_agent_session beyond whole-record atomic writes?
How does the live supervisor set and update state so the importer can safely distinguish live from closed records?
Are agent-*.jsonl sidechains and subagents/ files imported by a different organ, and where is that code?
What is the 'guide' referenced in the docstring, and where are criteria F1.2, B2, C6, and F10.1 defined?
What should happen if a jsonl file has no timestamp lines at all?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: it reads the catalog read-only, builds closed registry records using the documented title chain, skips live and unchanged records, and reports coverage and errors.
intention_signals: Backfill the entire historical Claude Code session catalog into a durable registry so it can be listed and resolved, while leaving live session ownership and the source catalog untouched.
novelty: The explicit no-events backfill law and the additive summarizer flag that distinguishes CC-internal summary one-shots from Tim's actual sessions.
contribution: A read-only, idempotent, event-free importer that transforms the ~/.claude/projects jsonl catalog into closed agent-session registry records, complete with a deterministic title fallback chain and a machine-session marker.
summary_for_embedding: A Python CLI importer that backfills closed Claude Code session records from ~/.claude/projects jsonl files into a company store registry using a streaming, read-only, event-free, idempotent process.
intention_for_embedding: To make the entire historical Claude Code session catalog discoverable via the registry without disturbing live session state or emitting fabric events.

symbols.does: Extracts the first text content from a message dict, handling plain string content or the first text block in a list.
symbols.description: Normalizes Claude Code message content so downstream title extraction sees a single string; it ignores non-text blocks and missing content.

symbols.does: Collapses whitespace and hard-caps a string to TITLE_MAX characters for use as a session title.
symbols.description: Turns multi-line or irregular text into a single-line catalog label, appending an ellipsis when truncation is required.

symbols.does: Streams a main-session jsonl once and returns timestamps, metadata, title candidates, turn count, bad lines, and a summarizer flag.
symbols.description: Performs the one read-only pass over a potentially large jsonl file; overwrites ai_title and last_prompt on each encounter to achieve last-occurrence-wins semantics without a second tail scan.

symbols.does: Converts the scan result into a single registry record using the exact title fallback chain and file stat metadata.
symbols.description: Applies the documented ai-title, last-prompt, first-user-turn, untitled-envelope priority, sets state to closed, and records provenance fields such as jsonl_bytes, jsonl_mtime, and title_source.

symbols.does: Walks the projects directory, filters main session files, applies skip/duplicate rules, writes records, and accumulates statistics.
symbols.description: The core backfill loop: it enforces idempotency via bytes+mtime, protects non-closed live records, resolves cross-project duplicate UUIDs by last_activity, and collects per-file errors.

symbols.does: Prints a human-readable import report including counts, title-source percentages, summarizer share, and file errors.
symbols.description: Formats the stats dict into the coverage report required by the F1.2 verification bar, making failures and title coverage explicit.

symbols.does: Parses arguments, validates the projects directory, instantiates the store, runs the import, prints stats, and returns an exit code.
symbols.description: CLI entry point that wires argparse to run_import and enforces the fail-loud contract: non-zero exit if file errors occurred or nothing was imported.
